extracted_facts: 3 fascia layers: superficial investing (platysma/EJV), pretracheal (continuous with carotid sheath, superior/inf thyroid vessels), prevertebral (thick, lines prevert muscles, sympathetic trunk on surface).
Landmarks: angle of mandible ~C2-3, carotid tubercle C6.
Planes: platysma (VII), SCM (XI) retract lateral, strap muscles (C1-3) medial, longus colli (segmental cervical nn).
Deep: split longus colli and ALL in midline, subperiosteal expose, retract longus/ALL laterally, needle localize level.
Risks: RLN (~2.3%, right more variable/vulnerable); sympathetic chain on longus colli lateral at C6 (Horner if injured); carotid sheath; retropharyngeal hematoma.

title: Olecranon Fractures - Trauma - Orthobullets
extracted_facts: Osteology: olecranon + coronoid form greater sigmoid (semilunar) notch articulating with trochlea for flexion-extension + stability.
Muscles: triceps inserts posterior proximal ulna (blends periosteum, radial n C7); anconeus lateral olecranon (radial C7).

ORIF: tension band (transverse no comminution) converts distraction to compression (K-wires engage ant cortex risk AIN/forearm rotation); dorsal plate for comminuted/Monteggia/oblique distal to coronoid; IMN with tension band.
Excision + triceps advancement: salvage elderly <50% joint, osteoporotic, nonunion (decreased extension strength, instability risk if ligs missed).
Posterior approach (12005): midline post incision curve around olecranon; identify/protect ulnar n (palp 2cm prox med epic); V-osteotomy 2cm from tip (pre-drill/tap); subperiosteal med/lat humerus.

Dangers posterior elbow: ulnar n (protect, no transposition benefit shown); median n (strict subperiosteal ant humerus, flex elbow relaxes); radial n (prox lat intermuscular septum); brachial a (with median).
